title: Exogenous event — COVID as 9/11-style shock with dot-com bubble underlay
html:
Exogenous event — COVID as 9/11-style shock with dot-com bubble underlay

Source: 2020-03-24 Apple Note. ~80-word macro thesis from 17-year-old Yahir, eight days into the U.S. COVID lockdown. Frames COVID as an exogenous shock layered onto a market already underlined with bubble conditions — like 9/11 hitting the economy mid-dot-com unwind. Predicts gov't stimulus works temporarily, then doesn't, then a major downturn before a gradual return-to-normal.

TL;DR

  • Yahir labels COVID an "exogenous event" and analogizes it to 9/11 hitting an already-frothy market — the dot-com bubble parallel as the underlying-condition layer.
  • Predicts: stimulus works short-term → fails → major downturn → recovery only when "life returns to normal," and that return will be gradual, not switch-flip.
  • Captures Yahir's earliest crisp macro-as-narrative thinking — sets up the 2020 03 30 balance-sheet watchlist that follows six days later.
  • Single PDF/image attachment hinted at (￼) but not extracted; likely a chart screenshot from his 17-year-old stock-research workflow.
  • The terse, principle-first framing ("Shit is going to get worse before it gets better") foreshadows the manifesto style note form Yahir uses through 2022.

Pattern observed

This is the seed-document for Yahir's post pandemic futurism cluster. The "exogenous event + pre-existing fragility" framing recurs in his COVID era notes and arguably maps onto how he later thinks about AI search as an exogenous shock to SEO that hits an already-crowded market.

Open questions

  • Did Yahir actually read about the dot-com bubble, or is the analogy borrowed from Curzio Research / financial-media diet at the time?
  • The "ropes conditions" phrase reads garbled — likely "frothy conditions" via dictation. Confirm in any later use.

Related

  • post pandemic future thinking
  • 2020 03 30 facebook

related.label: FACEBOOK — early-COVID stock thesis, future-of-work futurism, and a Mrs. Ransom dream
